claim: The universal sensorium hooks (SessionStart/PostCompact gen_spec, UserPromptSubmit emit_cipher+claude_md_diff_watch+attention_hook, PreToolUse graph-guard, Stop context_producer) shall live in a committed project settings.json generated by a setup tool with commands portable via $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, never only in the personal git-ignored settings.local.json.
why: Not yet implemented: setup-hooks is Planned in methodology.Tools, and the adapter tools it would wire (attention-hook, claude-md-diff-watch, context-producer, emit-cipher) are all Planned as well. The committed-portable-sensorium generator does not exist: a committed .claude/settings.json exists in the repo, but it is not produced by a setup tool and does not carry the full sensorium hook enumeration. (Historical note: the original generator was tools/setup_hooks.py, which produced the committed project settings.json so that a fresh clone got the pulse and the graph-guard rather than starting blind.) The original WHY stands: hooks that live only in the personal git-ignored settings.local.json leave a fresh clone with no pulse and no graph-guard. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.
